data.title: Slovak economy maintains growth in Q1
data.description:
Slovakia's economy grew by 0.2% quarter-on-quarter in the first quarter, preliminary data showed on Thursday.

On an annual basis, gross domestic product increased by 0.9%, below a forecast of 1.2% in a Reuters poll and under 1% for the first time in two years, the statistics office said.

The office said the year-on-year GDP increase was supported by domestic demand although household consumption growth slowed. Investment remained in a year-on-year decline, it said.

data.title: AMERICAN SALARS ADDS LITHIUM BRINE EXPERT DR. MARK KING AS A TECHNCIAL ADVISOR AND QUALIFIED PERSON
data.description:
VANCOUVER, BC, May 15, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — AMERICAN SALARS LITHIUM INC. ("AMERICAN SALARS" OR THE "COMPANY") (CSE: USLI, OTC: ASALF, FWB: Z3P, WKN: A3E2NY) announces the addition of Dr. Mark King PhD, PGeo, FGC, a world-renowned lithium brine expert, as a Technical Advisor and Qualified Person.

Dr. King is a hydrogeologist with 30+ years of international experience in groundwater modeling and geochemistry. For the past 15 years, he has specialized in exploration and evaluation of lithium brine projects. His strong chemistry and numerical modeling background has proven to be an excellent foundation for brine exploration and quantitative evaluation. Consequently, his resource and reserve estimation experience on major brine projects is now arguably the most extensive of any geologist, hydrogeologist, or engineer in the world.

Some notable past involvements include serving as a resource and/or reserve estimation Qualified Person for the following:

  • Albermarle at Salar Atacama (Chile), Silver Peak (Nevada, USA) and Antofalla Salar (Argentina)
  • Neo Lithium at the 3Q Salar, (Argentina)
  • Lithium Americas at the Cauchari Salar, (Argentina)
  • Vulcan Energy in the Rhine Valley, (Germany)
  • Alpha Lithium at Tolillar & Hombre Muerto Salar, (Argentina)

In addition, Dr. King and his team have conducted detailed due diligence reviews of 20+ advanced brine projects and reconnaissance reviews (and ranking) of 100+ greenfield to early-stage projects, in South America and the southern US. His technical team at GWI have advanced expertise in geological modelling, GIS, data management and 3D visualization. They will provide exploration and resource consulting services to American Salars from time to time.

R. Nick Horsley, CEO & Director States, “American Salars is yet again adding depth to its technical team. We are fortunate to welcome Dr. King and his team at GWI to American Salars and look forward to working together in our search for significant lithium salar projects. Mark is a globally recognized authority whose work has taken him to lithium brine projects throughout North and South America, and beyond.”

About American Salars Lithium Inc.

About American Salars Lithium Inc. American Salars Lithium Inc. is an exploration company focused on exploring and developing high-value battery metals projects to meet the demands of the advancing electric vehicle market.

All Stakeholders are encouraged to follow the Company on its social media profiles on LinkedIn, Twitter and Instagram.

On Behalf of the Board of Directors,

“R. Nick Horsley”

R. Nick Horsley, CEO

For further information, please contact:

American Salars Lithium Inc.

‎Phone: 604.880.2189

‎E-Mail: info@americansalars.com

‎

Neither the Canadian Securities Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the CSE) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Disclaimer for Forward-Looking Information

Certain statements in this release are forward-looking statements, which reflect the expectations of management regarding American Salar’s intention to continue to identify potential transactions and make certain corporate changes and applications. Forward looking statements consist of statements that are not purely historical, including any statements regarding beliefs, plans, expectations, or intentions regarding the future. Such statements are subject to risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results, performance, or developments to differ materially from those contained in the statements. No assurance can be given that any of the events anticipated by the forward-looking statements will occur or, if they do occur, what benefits American Salars will obtain from them. These forward-looking statements reflect managements’ current views and are based on certain expectations, estimates and assumptions which may prove to be incorrect. A number of risks and uncertainties could cause actual results to differ materially from those expressed or implied by the forward-looking statements, including American Salars results of exploration or review of properties that American Salars does acquire. These forward-looking statements are made as of the date of this news release and American Salars assumes no obligation to update these forward-looking statements, or to update the reasons why actual results differed from those projected in the forward-looking statements, except in accordance with applicable securities laws.

data.title: Safello increases exposure to TAO in its strategic crypto reserve
data.description:
Safello

Stockholm, 15 May 2025 | Safello, the leading cryptocurrency exchange in the Nordics, today announces a second reallocation of its crypto treasury, further increasing exposure to TAO, the native token of the decentralized AI protocol Bittensor.

On 14 May 2025, Safello sold 1 BTC and acquired 229.2385 TAO at 402 EUR per TAO.

This follows Safello’s initial TAO acquisition on 8 May and is part of a longer-term dollar cost averaging strategy. Safello’s crypto treasury now consists of 8 BTC, 113 ETH, and 469,0385 TAO.

Safello sees decentralized AI as a defining shift for the next decade – one that will reshape how intelligence is created, distributed, and owned.

With this continued reallocation of its treasury, the company is aligning capital with conviction and taking deliberate steps to support open, permissionless AI infrastructure. This move is underpinned by a strong thesis that decentralized AI protocols like Bittensor will play a foundational role in the evolution of internet-scale intelligence.

###

For more information, please contact

David Leeb, Chief Marketing Officer, at press@safello.com

Certified Adviser

Amudova AB is Safello’s certified adviser.

Safello is the leading cryptocurrency exchange in the Nordics, with over 400,000 users. The company is empowering financial independence by making crypto accessible to everyone. Safello offers a secure and easy solution for buying, selling, storing, as well as depositing and withdrawing cryptocurrencies directly from the blockchain – ensuring seamless transactions at industry-leading speeds. Operating in Sweden, Safello has been registered as a financial institution with Finansinspektionen (Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority) since 2013 and is listed at Nasdaq First North Growth Market since 2021. For more information visit www.safello.com.

https://news.cision.com/safello/r/safello-increases-exposure-to-tao-in-its-strategic-crypto-reserve%2Cc4150836

data.title: Euro zone bond yields edge below multi-week highs ahead of data deluge
data.description:
Euro zone bond yields edged down on Thursday as traders await key U.S. data due later in the session for a steer on the world's biggest economy, while relief on easing trade tensions faded, pushing some flows into fixed income.

Germany's 10-year yield (DE10YT=RR), the euro area's benchmark, was down one basis point (bp) to 2.687%, but remained close to a multi-week high of 2.7% hit on Wednesday.

German 2-year yields (DE2YT=RR), more sensitive to changes in expectations for European Central Bank policy, were also off 1 bp at 1.934%.

Italy's 10-year yield were flat at 3.72% .

Closely-watched U.S. data on Thursday includes April retail sales and jobless figures due later.

Official figures showed Britain's economy grew by a better-than-expected 0.2% in March. Traders are also awaiting euro zone flash GDP figures for Q1 due later in morning trading.

data.title: U.K. Economy Raced at Start of Year But Slowdown Looms — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

The U.K. economy outpaced growth in the U.S. and the eurozone in the first three months of the year, though a slowdown likely lies ahead in an environment of rising joblessness and a pessimistic mood among consumers and businesses.

Gross domestic product expanded 0.7% in the quarter through March, figures from the U.K.'s Office for National Statistics showed Thursday. That was faster than economists' forecasts of 0.6% growth, and means Britain outpaced the U.S.--where the economy contracted--and the eurozone, which grew 0.4% over the period. On an annualized basis, GDP grew by 2.9%, the fastest rate since the beginning of last year. The expansion was fuelled by growth in the services sector, which is key to the U.K. economy.

The growth figures "show the strength and potential of the U.K. economy," government finance chief Rachel Reeves said in a post on X.

A strong start to the year is nonetheless likely to be a fleeting boost for the government, which says lifting the economy out of a long period of stagnation is its main priority. The U.K.'s economy had also started 2024 with a growth burst that faded in subsequent quarters, and economists expect to see a similar pattern this year.

"The bump higher in activity will likely be short-lived," Sanjay Raja, an economist at Deutsche Bank, wrote in a note to clients.

Among the hurdles ahead for the British economy is an increase in taxes on employers that came into effect at the start of the second quarter. Along with an increase in the minimum wage, that has damaged business confidence and unemployment is now gradually rising, figures from the ONS showed this week.

Businesses also face uncertainty about future taxes, with the government likely to have to raise rates as well as cut spending to meet self-imposed budget rules. The National Institute of Economic and Social Research, a leading research group, last week warned that the rules have become a perennial source of uncertainty that weakens investment and growth.

The global economy is in a difficult place, and the government has a tough fight on its hands to reinvigorate our sluggish economy in the midst of such uncertainty," said Joe Nellis, an economic adviser at consultancy MHA. "However, they would make this fight somewhat easier if they stopped tying their own hands behind their back and discarded their burdensome fiscal rules."

And despite rising real incomes as inflation cools, pessimistic Britons are keeping their purse strings tight. Consumer confidence dropped in April to its lowest point this year in an index compiled by research group GfK, and sales at retail stores are below the levels booked before the coronavirus pandemic.

Reflecting the strong start to the year, the Bank of England last week raised its growth forecast for this year. But it lowered its projection for 2026.

Despite weak economic growth, the central bank has been more cautious in cutting its key interest than the European Central Bank.

The U.K. has suffered from a long period of low productivity growth that partly reflects low levels of investment since the global financial crisis and its 2016 decision to leave the European Union.

A shrinking workforce, strained by an aging population and the state-run health system's struggle to cope with long-term illnesses, risks being compounded by government's tougher stance on immigration after recent electoral setbacks to the Reform UK party.

For the BOE, those problems raise questions about how rapidly the economy can grow without reaching capacity and generating fresh inflation pressures. Set against those concerns, changes to U.S. tariffs are a relatively minor issue.

"Tariffs are not the dominating thing for monetary policy in the U.K.," said Claire Lombardelli, the BOE's deputy governor responsible for monetary policy.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: ITV Revenue Falls on Lower Internal Studios Revenue
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

ITV reported lower first-quarter revenue due to a decline in internal studios revenue and said it expects margins to fall for the year.

The U.K. broadcaster on Thursday said revenue for the period was 875 million pounds, equivalent to $1.16 billion, compared with 887 million pounds the year before. Internal studios revenue fell 20%, hurt by some programs not returning and the year-over-year difference in the phasing of production of programs such as "The Bay" and "Grace."

ITV Studios revenue rose 1% to 386 million pounds and reflected the 20% rise in external revenue thanks to strong demand from global streaming platforms.

Advertising revenue fell 2% to 423 million pounds due to a strong comparative period last year, which benefited from the UEFA European Championship.

The company said it expects revenue, profit and margins to be second-half weighted. Still, while margins for the year are expected to be lower than in 2024--due to the change in sales mix as the market recovers following strikes in the U.K.--they will remain within the 13% to 15% target range.

The board said it continues to assess the effects of trade tariffs in the U.S. However, since it produces television programming, it said it doesn't expect any direct impact from the imposition of levies on films.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Dollar Falls on Renewed Speculation Trump Wants to Engineer Weaker Currency — Market Talk
data.description: The dollar falls amid speculation that President Trump could seek agreements with other countries to devalue the dollar as part of trade deals. Reports that the U.S. and South Korea discussed exchange rate polices last week "should be enough to set a few alarm bells ringing" and clearly did given the dollar's negative reaction, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. If the Trump administration was able to engineer a weaker dollar through deals with other trading partners, the Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut interest rates given the prospect of higher import costs, he says. The DXY falls 0.4% to 100.686. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Copper Prices Decline Amid Trade Uncertainty
data.description:
Copper futures fell to around $4.57 per pound on Thursday, extending losses for a second session as global trade uncertainties continued to pressure sentiment.

While a 90-day tariff reduction deal between the US and China marked a step toward de-escalation, analysts cautioned that the absence of a long-term agreement clouds the broader economic outlook.

US policymakers have also reiterated their aim to reduce reliance on Chinese imports, fueling concerns about sustained disruption in global supply chains.

Adding to the bearish tone, the International Copper Study Group doubled its 2025 global surplus forecast to nearly 300,000 tonnes, citing both rising production and weakened demand expectations.

The group added: “In view of uncertainty surrounding international trade policy that is likely to weaken the global economic outlook and negatively impact copper demand, usage growth rates have been revised down compared to the group’s September 2024 forecasts.”

data.title: CBOT soybeans to fall toward $10.54-1/4 to $10.59-3/4 range
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean July contract (SN25) is expected to fall towards the $10.54-1/4 to $10.59-3/4 range, as it has broken support at $10.65 per bushel.

The current fall is assumed to be against the short uptrend from $10.36-3/4. Five small waves make up the trend. The wave 4 ended around $10.54-1/4, which serves as a target.

A retracement analysis on the trend reveals a target zone of $10.54-1/4 to $10.59-3/4 as well. Resistance is at $10.71-3/4, a break above, which could lead to a gain into the $10.79-1/4 to $10.82-1/2 range.

On the daily chart, there was a false break above key resistance at $10.71-1/2, suggesting either a dissipation of the bullish momentum or a completion of a wave C from $9.85-1/4.

In either case, the market may retrace to $10.55.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Dentsu 1Q APAC Ex-Japan Adjusted Operating Loss Y3.22B Vs Y3.14B Loss a Year Earlier
Saudi Exchange Sets Fluctuation Limits for Yaqeen Capital's Capital Increase
data.description:
The Saudi Exchange set the fluctuation limits for Yaqeen Capital's (SASE:9602) capital increase through bonus shares following shareholder approval on Wednesday.

The limits will be based on the Saudi Arabian investment bank's share price of 12.58 Saudi riyals, with outstanding orders to be canceled, according to a Thursday release.

Saudi Arabia's Securities Depository Center, or Edaa, will add the shares to the portfolios of investors by May 19.

data.title: Iveco approves spinoff plan for defence unit as it considers its sale
data.description:
Italian truck and bus maker Iveco Group said on Thursday it will press ahead with a plan to spin off its defence business while exploring interest from potential buyers for the unit.

The group, controlled by Exor , the investment company of Italy's Agnelli family, said in February it was considering spinning off the unit, known as IDV, to simplify its structure and create greater flexibility for both businesses, adding it would provide an update in the following months.

The separation is expected to take place this year, the company said.

The group said it had received preliminary expressions of interest from potential strategic buyers for its defence business.

"The board has therefore mandated the management to continue the preparation for the spinoff, while exploring such preliminary interests," it added.

Leonardo , together with Germany's Rheinmetall , has filed a non-binding offer for IDV, the CEO of the Italian aerospace and defence said last week without providing further details. According to a media report earlier this week Spanish defence company Indra also sent a non-binding offer.

The announcement came as Iveco posted a 42% contraction of the adjusted operating profit of its industrial activities for the first quarter of the year, to 117 million euros ($131 million), due to lower truck demand in Europe.

The result was short of a 140 million euro analyst consensus provided by the company.

Iveco also confirmed its preliminary forecasts for its full-year results, including one for adjusted EBIT from industrial activities of between 850 and 900 million euros, with CEO Olof Persson mentioning a "strong order book" for the company.

($1 = 0.8937 euros)

data.title: Sapura Energy Auditors Flag Going Concern Warning
data.description:
Sapura Energy received a going-concern warning from its auditors after its liabilities exceeded assets by 4.31 billion ringgit as of end-January, according to a Wednesday Malaysian bourse filing.

The company is relying on the successful execution of its regularization plan, which includes court-approved creditor schemes, a 1.1 billion ringgit funding deal with Malaysia Development, and the completed sale of its SapuraOMV stake.

Sapura Energy aims to finalize the plan by August to exit PN17 status and stabilize its finances, it added.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hang Seng Bank Slashes Employee Headcount Amid HSBC's Massive Restructuring
data.description:
Hang Seng Bank is cutting the number of its employees as part of parent HSBC's restructuring efforts, the South China Morning Post reported Wednesday, citing two separate sources.

The bank started to inform employees across various departments, such as information technology and corporate communications, over the past few weeks regarding the layoffs, the report said.

Also included in the layoffs are staff from index compiler Hang Seng Indexes and other units that could be hit by consolidation, the report said.

The sources did not say how many would be affected by the cuts, but some departments lost up to one-fifth of their staff, with the most hit seeing their numbers halved, the SCMP said.

The layoffs were surprising as local banks in Hong Kong do not tend to lay off staff and keep their employee count stable even during previous financial crises, the report said, citing Everbright Securities International strategist Kenny Ng Lai-yin.

HSBC owns 62.14% of Hang Seng Bank.

An HSBC representative referred MT Newswires to Hang Seng Bank for direct comment.

Hang Seng Bank did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

data.title: India's Vascon Engineers climbs on quarterly profit surge, upbeat outlook
data.description:
** Shares of Vascon Engineers rise 16.6% to 52.1 rupees, on track for biggest one-day gain since January 2022

** Construction engineering company's net profit in the March quarter doubles y/y to 347.7 million rupees ($4.06 million)

** Revenue jumps 66%; company also logs its highest-ever annual income of 10.9 billion rupees in fiscal 2025

** With a robust order book worth 28.25 billion rupees and increased capacity utilization, profit growth momentum is likely to pick up further in fiscal 2026, VASC says

** 1.04 million shares change hands, nearly 11 times the 30-day average as of 12:14 p.m. IST, highest in 10 months

($1 = 85.5750 Indian rupees)

data.title: Al Wathba National Insurance's Q1 Net Loss Narrows; Insurance Revenue Up
data.description:
Al Wathba National Insurance (ADX:AWNIC) said Thursday that its net loss for the first quarter narrowed, while insurance revenue increased year over year.

Net loss for the three months ended March 31 was 16.1 million Emirati dirhams, compared with a net loss of 28.2 million dirhams a year ago.

The Abu Dhabi-listed insurance company's insurance revenue was 164.5 million dirhams, compared with 75 million dirhams earlier.
